persona.name: Marcus Thompson
persona.personality: A diligent IT service manager who is detail-oriented about aging incidents and SLA hygiene. He is direct, urgency-driven, and wants assignees clearly informed that their stale cases must now be handled ahead of everything else.

user_scenario.task_description: Please review all currently open incidents assigned to the group named Level 1 Support Team that were opened before 20 February 2024 and treat them as requiring the same level of attention as top-priority work. Send alerts to the users currently assigned to these incidents using the subject Priority Escalation for Aging Incidents, and make it clear in the message that the incident has been escalated as top-priority work due to how long it has remained unresolved, briefly reminding the assignee what the case is about by referencing its incident summary and also stating the date in which this incident was created, and emphasizing that this work should now be handled ahead of other tasks and driven to resolution as quickly as possible.

arguments.subject: Priority Escalation for Aging Incidents
arguments.message: This incident (printer connectivity issue) has been escalated to top priority because it has remained unresolved for too long since it was created on 2024-02-19. Please now handle it ahead of other tasks and drive it to resolution as quickly as possible, treating it as top-priority work to be closed out as soon as possible.

arguments.subject: Priority Escalation for Aging Incidents
arguments.message: This incident (network connectivity issues) has been escalated to top priority because it has remained unresolved for too long since it was created on 2024-02-18. Please now handle it ahead of other tasks and drive it to resolution as quickly as possible, treating it as top-priority work to be closed out as soon as possible.

nl_assertions: An alert notification with the subject 'Priority Escalation for Aging Incidents' is sent to Carlos Rodriguez (carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com), the assignee of the open printer connectivity incident INC_003 opened 2024-02-19.

An alert notification with the subject 'Priority Escalation for Aging Incidents' is sent to Elena Petrov (elena.petrov@techcorp.com), the assignee of the open network connectivity incident INC_004 opened 2024-02-18.

Each alert message states the incident was escalated as top priority because it has remained unresolved, references the incident summary, includes the incident creation date, and stresses it must be handled ahead of other tasks and resolved as quickly as possible.

Only the open incidents assigned to the Level 1 Support Team group that were opened before 20 February 2024 are escalated; resolved incidents and incidents opened on or after that date are not notified.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_003' AND email = 'carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com' AND type = 'alert' AND subject = 'Priority Escalation for Aging Incidents' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%escalated%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%unresolved%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%printer connectivity%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%ahead of other tasks%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%quick%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%top%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%priority%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%possible%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%19%'

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_004' AND email = 'elena.petrov@techcorp.com' AND type = 'alert' AND subject = 'Priority Escalation for Aging Incidents' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%escalated%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%unresolved%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%network connectivity%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%ahead of other tasks%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%quick%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%top%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%priority%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%possible%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%18%'

persona.name: Marcus Thompson
persona.personality: A decisive IT manager driving an org-wide team reorganization; concise, outcome-focused, and expects the assistant to execute the consolidation end to end without hand-holding.

user_scenario.task_description: The current manager requires a single, unified space to communicate with all members who previously belonged to the different Support teams (excluding 'IT Support Team' as it is working on a critical task and will be addressed later separately). As part of this shift, the existing Support teams should be formally dissolved and create a new consolidated group named 'Cloud Ops Alpha', categorized as 'IT Support' with the description 'Next-gen cloud infrastructure handling'. After establishing the new group, add all individuals from the former Support teams so they retain their communication channels and required access. Finally, ensure that any currently available manager is assigned to oversee the newly formed group.

nl_assertions: The former Support team 'Level 1 Support Team' (GROUP_001) is dissolved by being deactivated, while the 'IT Support Team' is left untouched.
A new active group named 'Cloud Ops Alpha' of type 'IT Support' is created with the description 'Next-gen cloud infrastructure handling'.
An available manager (Priya Sharma, USER_002) is assigned to oversee the newly created 'Cloud Ops Alpha' group.
All members of the dissolved former Support team(s) are added to 'Cloud Ops Alpha' so they retain their communication channels and access.

validation_config.query: select count() from user_group where group_id = 'GROUP_001' and active = 0
select count() from user_group where name = 'Cloud Ops Alpha' and type = 'IT Support' and active = 1 and description = 'Next-gen cloud infrastructure handling' and manager_id = 'USER_002'

select count() from user_group_member where group_id = ( select group_id from user_group where name = 'Cloud Ops Alpha' and type = 'IT Support' and active = 1 and description = 'Next-gen cloud infrastructure handling')

persona.name: Marcus Thompson
persona.personality: A decisive, process-driven IT service manager who enforces new escalation rules immediately and communicates with care toward both reporters and assignees.

user_scenario.task_description: The group whose type is IT Support and that is classified as a level 1 support team has adopted a new rule that must be applied today: the oldest incident that is still unresolved and currently assigned to this group must be escalated by setting its impact, urgency, and priority to their highest available levels and by sending reports both to the user who reported it and to the user who is currently assigned to resolve it. The report for the user who originally reported this incident needs to have the subject Good News: Your Incident Has Been Escalated for Immediate Resolution. In the message, greet this reporter by their full name and congratulate them, explaining that their incident has been promoted and will now be handled as a top priority instead of waiting in the normal queue. Make it clear that the expectation is for their incident to be fully resolved no later than tomorrow. On the other hand, the report for the user who is currently assigned to resolve this incident needs to have the subject Urgent Action Required: Highest-Priority Incident for Same-Day Focus. In this message, greet the assignee by their full name and clearly apologize that they must pause their other work and dedicate their full attention to this incident. Provide context by stating the current incident summary so they understand what the issue is about, and emphasize that they also have at most one day to resolve this incident, because the reporting user needs it fully fixed by tomorrow.

arguments.subject: Good News: Your Incident Has Been Escalated for Immediate Resolution
arguments.message: Dear Carlos Rodriguez, congratulations! We have great news for you. Your incident has been escalated and promoted to top priority. It will now be handled immediately as a top priority instead of waiting in the normal queue. Our expectation is that your incident will be fully resolved no later than tomorrow.

arguments.subject: Urgent Action Required: Highest-Priority Incident for Same-Day Focus
arguments.message: Dear Elena Petrov, we sincerely apologize, but we must ask you to pause your other work and dedicate your full attention to this incident. Context: the incident summary is 'Network connectivity issues'. This is now the highest-priority incident. You have at most one day to resolve it, because the reporting user needs it fully fixed by tomorrow.

nl_assertions: The oldest unresolved incident assigned to the IT Support level 1 support team (Level 1 Support Team) is escalated by setting its impact and urgency to high and its priority to critical.

A report notification is sent to the incident's reporter (Carlos Rodriguez, carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com) with the subject 'Good News: Your Incident Has Been Escalated for Immediate Resolution', greeting them by full name, congratulating them, and explaining the incident is now top priority instead of waiting in the queue, with the expectation it will be fully resolved by tomorrow.

A report notification is sent to the incident's assignee (Elena Petrov, elena.petrov@techcorp.com) with the subject 'Urgent Action Required: Highest-Priority Incident for Same-Day Focus', greeting them by full name, apologizing that they must pause other work and dedicate their full attention, stating the incident summary 'Network connectivity issues', and emphasizing it must be fixed within one day, by tomorrow.
The agent correctly identifies the target incident and recipients via the IT Support level 1 group rather than assuming any IDs.

verifiers.name: validate_incident_escalation_fields
verifiers.description: Check incident has highest impact, urgency, priority

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = 'INC_004' AND impact = 'high' AND urgency = 'high' AND priority = 'critical'

verifiers.name: validate_reporter_notification_escalated
verifiers.description: Verify reporter notification for escalated incident was sent

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_004' AND type = 'report' AND email = 'carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com' AND subject = 'Good News: Your Incident Has Been Escalated for Immediate Resolution' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%carlos rodriguez%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%congratulat%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%top priority%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%queue%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%expectat%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%full%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%resolve%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%tomorrow%'

verifiers.name: validate_assignee_notification_urgent
verifiers.description: Verify assignee notification for highest-priority incident was sent

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_004' AND type = 'report' AND email = 'elena.petrov@techcorp.com' AND subject = 'Urgent Action Required: Highest-Priority Incident for Same-Day Focus' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%elena petrov%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%network connectivity issues%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%apolog%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%pause%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%work%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%dedicate%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%attention%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%fixed%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%one day%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%tomorrow%'

persona.name: Marcus Thompson
persona.personality: An organized, decisive IT administrator who wants to streamline team structure efficiently and expects the consolidation to be carried out cleanly without back-and-forth.

user_scenario.task_description: At the moment, we need a single combined group to work together with all members from different teams after dissolving those teams. There are two separate teams working at the same site: one called 'IT Support Team' that handles day-to-day user and service support, and another called 'Infrastructure Team' that looks after backend systems and platform stability. The new consolidated group should named 'Unified Reliability Squad', categorised as 'IT Support'. Add everyone who belongs to those team groups and assign any active manager to oversee this newly created group.

arguments.name: Unified Reliability Squad
arguments.description: Consolidated reliability group merging the IT Support Team and Infrastructure Team.

nl_assertions: A new active user group named 'Unified Reliability Squad' of type 'IT Support' is created and overseen by an active manager.
Every member of the old 'IT Support Team' (GROUP_003) and 'Infrastructure Team' (GROUP_004) is added as a member of the new consolidated group.
Both the old 'IT Support Team' and 'Infrastructure Team' groups are deactivated (dissolved).
The agent confirms the consolidation by reporting the new group and its members back to the user.

verifiers.name: New unified group created with correct properties
verifiers.description: Verify that new unified group created with correct properties

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM user_group WHERE name = 'Unified Reliability Squad' AND type = 'IT Support' AND manager_id IN ('USER_002', 'USER_008') AND active = 1;

verifiers.name: Old support and infrastructure teams have been deactivated
verifiers.description: Verify that old support and infrastructure teams have been deactivated

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM user_group WHERE group_id IN ('GROUP_003', 'GROUP_004') AND active = 0;

verifiers.name: All members from the old groups are now members of the new unified group
verifiers.description: Verify that all members from the old groups are now members of the new unified group

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM user_group_member WHERE group_id = 'GROUP_005' AND user_id IN ('USER_009', 'USER_008');

persona.name: Randy Martinez
persona.personality: A pragmatic L2 field support manager who is concise and process-driven, keeping callers informed and keeping incident records accurate.

user_scenario.task_description: This incident INC-000068 has been in the L2 Field Support team queue for some time now awaiting action from the caller. The laptop reported as not powering on is currently on hold until the caller brings in the device for further hardware checks, after which the required parts can be identified and arranged. Notify the caller through written communication for the same, and update the urgency to high.

nl_assertions: The urgency of incident INC-000068 is raised to high (and its priority becomes critical given the high impact).
A written notification of type reminder is queued to the caller's email asking them to bring in the laptop for further hardware checks.
The notification is associated with incident INC-000068 and is sent to the affected caller, Bethany Barnes.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = "INC_068" AND urgency = "high" AND priority = "critical";

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE notification_id = "NOTIF_031" AND incident_id = "INC_068" AND email = "bethany.barnes43@russelljohnsonandclark.com" AND type = "reminder" AND status = "queued";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = "INC_068" AND urgency = "high" AND priority = "critical";

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE notification_id = "NOTIF_031" AND incident_id = "INC_068" AND email = "bethany.barnes43@russelljohnsonandclark.com" AND type = "reminder" AND status = "queued";

persona.name: Priya Sharma
persona.personality: A diligent, results-oriented IT service manager who follows ITIL process carefully, keeps affected users informed, and pushes pending incidents toward quick resolution.

user_scenario.task_description: After identifying that incident INC0000011 is still pending in our workspace which recently was again brought to our notice by Carlos Rodriguez as it's highly impacting his Wi-Fi networks, indicating a high business impact that should be updated accordingly. Progress the work on this incident so as to get it quickly resolved, check if there are any SLAs already linked, if not provide a valid and appropriate SLA and align it to the incident. Also notify Carlos regarding the same.

arguments.subject: Update on your incident INC0000011 (Wi-Fi connection drops)
arguments.message: Hi Carlos, your incident INC0000011 has been escalated to high impact and is now in progress. An appropriate resolution SLA has been aligned and we are actively working toward a quick resolution.

nl_assertions: Incident INC0000011 (INC_016) is progressed to in_progress with its business impact raised to high and the resulting priority set to high.
An appropriate resolution SLA is linked to the incident since none existed before, using the high-priority resolution SLA definition with an in_progress stage.
Carlos Rodriguez is notified about the update to his incident via an update-type notification to his email.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = "INC_016" AND status = "in_progress" AND impact = "high" AND priority = "high";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident_sla WHERE incident_sla_id = "TSLA_011" AND incident_id = "INC_016" AND sla_def_id = "SLA_002" AND stage = "in_progress";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE notification_id = "NOTIF_008" AND incident_id = "INC_016" AND email = "carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com" AND type = "update";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = "INC_016" AND status = "in_progress" AND impact = "high" AND priority = "high";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident_sla WHERE incident_sla_id = "TSLA_011" AND incident_id = "INC_016" AND sla_def_id = "SLA_002" AND stage = "in_progress";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE notification_id = "NOTIF_008" AND incident_id = "INC_016" AND email = "carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com" AND type = "update";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = "INC_016" AND status = "in_progress" AND impact = "high" AND priority = "high";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident_sla WHERE incident_sla_id = "TSLA_011" AND incident_id = "INC_016" AND sla_def_id = "SLA_002" AND stage = "in_progress";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE notification_id = "NOTIF_008" AND incident_id = "INC_016" AND email = "carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com" AND type = "update";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = "INC_016" AND status = "in_progress" AND impact = "high" AND priority = "high";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident_sla WHERE incident_sla_id = "TSLA_011" AND incident_id = "INC_016" AND sla_def_id = "SLA_002" AND stage = "in_progress";
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE notification_id = "NOTIF_008" AND incident_id = "INC_016" AND email = "carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com" AND type = "update";

persona.name: Raj Patel
persona.personality: A diligent, customer-focused IT service manager who follows up closely on recurring user issues and communicates with empathy and clear, complete detail to colleagues.

user_scenario.task_description: Isabella Rossi reported an email sync issue yesterday that was marked as resolved, but she just contacted us to say the problem is happening again. Find her most recent resolved incident and reopen it so the team can investigate further. Send a notification to the agent who originally resolved it (subject: 'Reopened Incident: Further Action Required on Your Previous Resolution') explaining that the issue has recurred and asking them to try a different approach this time. Make sure to greet them by their full name, thank them for their previous work, remind them what the incident was about and what they did to resolve it, and explain that a permanent fix will help Isabella Rossi complete her daily tasks without further disruption.

arguments.subject: Reopened Incident: Further Action Required on Your Previous Resolution
arguments.message: Hi Kenji Tanaka, thank you for your previous work on this email sync incident. You had marked it resolved after a mobile device reconfiguration, but the issue has recurred for Isabella Rossi and we have reopened the incident. Could you please try a different approach this time, as the previous reconfiguration did not provide a lasting fix? A permanent resolution will help Isabella Rossi complete her daily tasks without further disruption. Thank you again for taking another look and finding a different, durable approach to resolve this.

nl_assertions: Isabella Rossi's most recent resolved incident (INC_022, the email sync issue) is reopened and set back to in_progress.

A notification is sent to Kenji Tanaka (kenji.tanaka@acme.com), the agent who originally resolved the incident, with the subject 'Reopened Incident: Further Action Required on Your Previous Resolution'.

The notification greets Kenji by his full name, thanks him for his previous work, recalls that the incident was about email sync and was resolved via reconfiguration, and asks him to try a different approach.
The notification explains that a permanent fix will help Isabella Rossi complete her daily tasks without further disruption.

verifiers.name: verify_incident_reopened_in_progress
verifiers.description: Check that Isabella's latest incident was reopened and is now in progress

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = 'INC_022' AND status = 'in_progress' AND caller_id = 'USER_010'

verifiers.name: verify_reopen_notification_sent_to_resolver
verifiers.description: Check that a reopen notification was queued for Kenji

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_022' AND email = 'kenji.tanaka@acme.com' AND type = 'report' AND subject = 'Reopened Incident: Further Action Required on Your Previous Resolution' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%kenji tanaka%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%thank%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%previous%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%email sync%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%reconfiguration%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%isabella rossi%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%approach%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%different%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%resolve%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%daily task%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%disruption%'

persona.name: Karen Watkins
persona.personality: Organized and forward-planning IT operations lead; gives detailed, batch instructions and expects everything set up correctly in advance for a smooth day one.

user_scenario.task_description: We're setting up a new Service Desk team for our India operations and need to get four new members into the system. Arjun Mehta (arjun.mehta@company.com, +91-98101-00011, role Agent) and Priya Nair (priya.nair@company.com, +91-98101-00012, role Agent) both joined today and need to be ready to start handling tickets immediately. In addition, Rohan Sharma (rohan.sharma@company.com, +91-98101-00013, role Reporter) and Neha Iyer (neha.iyer@company.com, +91-98101-00014, role Manager), who will be joining fifteen days from now, their accounts should still be created now so everything is set up in advance. Once all four user profiles have been created, create a new user group named "India Service Desk" with the group type "Service Desk" and the description "Frontline IT support for India offices." Add Arjun, Priya, Rohan, and Neha as members of this India Service Desk group, and set Neha Iyer as the manager of the group so that the team structure and access are fully ready for day one.

arguments.name: India Service Desk
arguments.description: Frontline IT support for India offices.

nl_assertions: Four new users are created: Arjun Mehta and Priya Nair as active agents, and Rohan Sharma (reporter) and Neha Iyer (manager) as inactive accounts since they join in fifteen days.
A new user group named 'India Service Desk' of type 'Service Desk' with the description 'Frontline IT support for India offices.' is created and active, with Neha Iyer set as its manager.
All four new users (Arjun, Priya, Rohan, and Neha) are added as members of the India Service Desk group.
The agent confirms the India Service Desk team structure and access are fully set up and ready for day one.

verifiers.name: Users created with correct roles and active/inactive flags
verifiers.description: Verify that the users are created with correct roles and active/inactive flags

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM users WHERE (first_name='Arjun' AND last_name='Mehta' AND email='arjun.mehta@company.com' AND role='agent' AND active=TRUE) OR (first_name='Priya' AND last_name='Nair' AND email='priya.nair@company.com' AND role='agent' AND active=TRUE) OR (first_name='Rohan' AND last_name='Sharma' AND email='rohan.sharma@company.com' AND role='reporter' AND active=FALSE) OR (first_name='Neha' AND last_name='Iyer' AND email='neha.iyer@company.com' AND role='manager' AND active=FALSE);

verifiers.name: India Service Desk group created
verifiers.description: Verify that India Service Desk group is created with Neha as a manager.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM user_group g JOIN users u ON g.manager_id = u.user_id WHERE g.name = 'India Service Desk' AND g.type = 'Service Desk' AND g.description = 'Frontline IT support for India offices.' AND g.active = TRUE;

verifiers.name: All four users are members of "India Service Desk"
verifiers.description: Verify that all four users are members of "India Service Desk"

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT u.email) AS cnt FROM user_group_member gm JOIN user_group g ON gm.group_id = g.group_id JOIN users u ON gm.user_id = u.user_id WHERE g.name = 'India Service Desk' AND g.type = 'Service Desk' AND u.email IN ( 'arjun.mehta@company.com', 'priya.nair@company.com', 'rohan.sharma@company.com', 'neha.iyer@company.com' );

persona.name: Karen Watkins
persona.personality: A focused, detail-oriented incident coordinator who relays a manager's instructions precisely and wants the major-incident response wrapped up cleanly without duplicated troubleshooting effort.

user_scenario.task_description: Gregory Richards is finalizing the response to that SAP Database Unreachable major incident. He spotted two symptomatic reports that need consolidation: one mentioning HR DB and another for Disk Full. Please find these and link them as children to the major SAP database incident. To ensure the desk doesn't duplicate troubleshooting, put that HR DB ticket on hold while we wait for the root problem resolution. Finally, he needs a Post-Outage Compliance Audit logged. Assign it to the same manager who's already handling the main database outage and link this audit as a child of the major incident as well.

nl_assertions: The HR DB incident (INC_057) and the Disk Full incident (INC_030) are linked as child incidents of the major SAP Database Unreachable incident INC_001.
The HR DB incident (INC_057) is placed on hold with the on-hold reason 'Awaiting Problem' so the desk does not duplicate troubleshooting while the root problem is resolved.
A new 'Post-Outage Compliance Audit' incident is created in the database category and assigned to Gregory Richards (USER_059), the manager already handling the main database outage.
The newly created Post-Outage Compliance Audit incident is also linked as a child of the major incident INC_001.

verifiers.name: child_incident_mappings_creation
verifiers.description: Verifies that three child incident mappings were created linking INC_057, INC_030, and INC_101 as children of parent incident INC_001.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM child_incident WHERE parent_incident = 'INC_001' AND child_incident IN ('INC_057', 'INC_030', 'INC_101') AND child_incident_mapping_id IN ('CINC_007', 'CINC_008', 'CINC_009');

verifiers.name: incident_inc_057_status_update
verifiers.description: Verifies that incident INC_057 was updated to on_hold status with Awaiting Problem as the hold reason.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = 'INC_057' AND status = 'on_hold' AND on_hold_reason = 'Awaiting Problem';

verifiers.name: incident_inc_101_creation
verifiers.description: Verifies that incident INC_101 was created with correct attributes for the post-outage compliance audit.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = 'INC_101' AND number = 'INC-000101' AND caller_id = 'USER_059' AND assigned_to = 'USER_059' AND short_description = 'Post-Outage Compliance Audit' AND category = 'database' AND status = 'new' AND impact = 'low' AND urgency = 'low' AND priority = 'planning';

verifiers.name: combined_all_operations_validation
verifiers.description: Comprehensive verifier that confirms all database operations were executed successfully, including child mappings, incident update, and incident creation.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT child_incident_mapping_id FROM child_incident WHERE parent_incident = 'INC_001' AND child_incident IN ('INC_057', 'INC_030', 'INC_101') AND child_incident_mapping_id IN ('CINC_007', 'CINC_008', 'CINC_009') UNION ALL SELECT incident_id FROM incident WHERE incident_id = 'INC_057' AND status = 'on_hold' AND on_hold_reason = 'Awaiting Problem' UNION ALL SELECT incident_id FROM incident WHERE incident_id = 'INC_101' AND number = 'INC-000101' AND caller_id = 'USER_059' AND assigned_to = 'USER_059' AND short_description = 'Post-Outage Compliance Audit' AND status = 'new') AS all_operations;

verifiers.name: parent_child_relationship_integrity
verifiers.description: Verifies that parent incident INC_001 has exactly three child incidents linked to it.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM child_incident WHERE parent_incident = 'INC_001';

verifiers.name: audit_trail_consistency
verifiers.description: Verifies that all child-incident relationships share the same organization and were created.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT() FROM (SELECT 1 FROM child_incident c JOIN incident p ON c.parent_incident = p.incident_id JOIN incident ch ON c.child_incident = ch.incident_id WHERE c.parent_incident = 'INC_001' AND c.child_incident IN ('INC_057', 'INC_030', 'INC_101') AND p.org_id = ch.org_id AND p.org_id = 'ORG_001' GROUP BY c.parent_incident HAVING COUNT() = 3) AS integrity_check;

persona.name: Marcus Thompson
persona.personality: A detail-oriented IT administrator handling an FY hardware-upgrade rollout; provides asset specifics up front and expects records to be created accurately and efficiently.

user_scenario.task_description: Aisha's newly issued corporate desktop (serial number DESKTOP-IN-781), procured for approximately INR 78 grand, was provided to her as part of the FY hardware upgrade initiative. She is currently working from the Bengaluru office in Karnataka, and the asset should be recorded under TechCorp India operations, with the status set to in use. The system is configured with an AMD Ryzen 7 processor, 32GB RAM, a 1TB SSD, and enhanced graphics support for development work. Please add this device to the item records. Additionally, she would like to raise an incident for a required software installation, with the impact set to medium, but it is not business critical.

arguments.short_description: Software installation required on new corporate desktop

nl_assertions: A new location for the TechCorp India Bengaluru office (Bengaluru, Karnataka, India) is created so the asset can be recorded under the correct location.
Aisha Williams's new corporate desktop is registered as a configuration item with serial number DESKTOP-IN-781, owner USER_004, cost 78000, and status in_use at the Bengaluru location.
An incident is raised for the required software installation on Aisha's new desktop, with caller USER_004, the new configuration item linked, impact medium, urgency low, and status new.
The agent confirms the asset and incident records were created and reports their identifiers back to the user.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM incident WHERE incident_id = "INC_024" AND configuration_item = "CI_005" AND caller_id = "USER_004" AND status = "new" AND impact = "medium" AND urgency = "low";

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM configuration_item WHERE configuration_item_id = "CI_005" AND serial_number = "DESKTOP-IN-781" AND owner_id = "USER_004" AND location_id = "LOC_004" AND status = "in_use" AND cost = 78000;

persona.name: Marcus Thompson
persona.personality: An organized, courteous ITSM administrator who follows up diligently and communicates clearly and warmly with both the affected users and the agents who do the work.

user_scenario.task_description: Please work with the most recently resolved incident handled by a group named Level 1 Support Team and send reports to the main participants. For the user who reported the incident, use the subject Incident Resolved - Service and Device Ready for Use, and a message clearly stating that their incident has been fully resolved, naming the associated service and item, and confirming that everything is now ready for proper use so they can continue their normal tasks. For the user who resolved the incident, use the subject Thank You for Resolving Recent Service Desk Incident, thanking them for their work, briefly restating what the incident summary described, and referencing the impact, urgency, and priority that were recorded, and noting that now that this case is closed they can expect to be assigned another incident within the next few hours.

arguments.subject: Incident Resolved - Service and Device Ready for Use
arguments.message: Hello Aisha, we are pleased to inform you that your incident has been fully resolved. The associated Email Service and the Carlos Laptop item are now fully operational and ready for proper use. Everything is now ready so that you can continue your normal tasks without any further interruption.

arguments.subject: Thank You for Resolving Recent Service Desk Incident
arguments.message: Hello Carlos, thank you for resolving this recent service desk incident. The incident summary described that the Keyboard keys not responding. The recorded impact was low, the urgency was low, and the resulting priority was low. Now that this case is closed, you can expect to be assigned another incident within the next few hours.

nl_assertions: Two report notifications are sent for the most recently resolved Level 1 Support Team incident (INC_017): one to the reporter (Aisha Williams) and one to the resolver (Carlos Rodriguez).

The reporter's notification uses the subject 'Incident Resolved - Service and Device Ready for Use' and states the incident is fully resolved, names the Email Service and the Carlos Laptop item, and confirms everything is ready so they can continue their normal tasks.

The resolver's notification uses the subject 'Thank You for Resolving Recent Service Desk Incident', thanks Carlos, restates the incident summary (keyboard keys not responding), and references the recorded impact, urgency, and priority (all low).
The resolver's notification notes that now the case is closed they can expect to be assigned another incident within the next few hours.

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_017' AND email = 'aisha.williams@techcorp.com' AND type = 'report' AND subject = 'Incident Resolved - Service and Device Ready for Use' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%email service%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%carlos laptop%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%resolved%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%ready%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%continue%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%tasks%'

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notification WHERE incident_id = 'INC_017' AND email = 'carlos.rodriguez@techcorp.com' AND type = 'report' AND subject = 'Thank You for Resolving Recent Service Desk Incident' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%thank%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%keyboard keys not responding%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%impact%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%urgency%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%priority%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%low%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%close%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%assigned%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%incident%' AND LOWER(message) LIKE '%few hours%'

persona.name: Marcus Thompson
persona.personality: An organized IT administrator who relays a problem manager's requirements precisely and expects records to be tracked formally and traceably according to ITIL practice.

user_scenario.task_description: A remote employee has been experiencing a recurring incident of VPN connection failures when connecting from home offices; the incident was originally assigned to Kenji Tanaka. The Infrastructure Problem Manager, Raj Patel, wants this recurring VPN issue to be formally tracked as a high impact network Problem. The problem should be created with status new, urgency medium, and a short description summarising the recurring VPN connection failures for remote users. He also wants a high impact new Change raised; the change should be created with short description "VPN gateway firmware upgrade and configuration hardening", priority high, and risk medium. This change must be mapped to the new Problem. As the fix is in progress the Problem should be updated with a workaround that notes a temporary mitigation for users.

arguments.problem_statement: Remote users connecting from home offices repeatedly experience VPN connection failures; recurring pattern originally tracked under incident INC_011 (assigned to Kenji Tanaka). Requires formal problem tracking and a permanent fix.
arguments.short_description: Recurring VPN connection failures for remote users from home offices
VPN gateway firmware upgrade and configuration hardening

arguments.workaround: Temporary mitigation: advise affected remote users to reconnect using the backup VPN gateway profile and switch from UDP to TCP transport until the gateway firmware upgrade is completed.

nl_assertions: A high impact, medium urgency network problem is created for the recurring VPN connection failures affecting remote users, linked to the originating incident INC_011 with priority high.
A new high impact, high priority, medium risk change request titled 'VPN gateway firmware upgrade and configuration hardening' is created.
The new change request is mapped to the newly created VPN problem for traceability.
The problem is updated with a workaround describing a temporary mitigation for users and its status is moved to fix_in_progress.

verifiers.name: VPN Problem created and updated correctly
verifiers.description: Verify that VPN Problem created and updated correctly

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM problem WHERE status = 'fix_in_progress' AND impact = 'high' AND urgency = 'medium' AND category = 'network' AND original_task = 'INC_011' AND priority = 'high';

verifiers.name: Change created for VPN gateway upgrade
verifiers.description: Verify that Change created for VPN gateway is upgrade

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM change WHERE short_description = 'VPN gateway firmware upgrade and configuration hardening' AND status = 'new' AND impact = 'high' AND risk = 'medium' AND priority = 'high';

verifiers.name: Change-Problem mapping exists
verifiers.description: Verify that Change-Problem have a mapping

validation_config.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM change_request_mapping m JOIN change c ON m.change_id = c.change_id JOIN problem p ON m.problem_id = p.problem_id WHERE c.short_description = 'VPN gateway firmware upgrade and configuration hardening';
